one_line_thesis: Enterprise asset intelligence compounder (CF + AVA segments); FY2025 FCF ~$16.23/sh; synthesis 13.15% at ~$275 → watch (below 15% hurdle).

deep_dive.executive_summary: Zebra is an enterprise asset intelligence compounder with a razor-and-blade model across mobile computing, printing, RFID, and growing services and software attach. FY2025 free cash flow per share is about $16.23 on $917 million of operating cash flow and $86 million of capital spending. The company repurchased $587 million of stock while closing the $1.30 billion Elo acquisition and the $62 million Photoneo deal, leaving cash at $125 million and long-term debt at $2.36 billion. Q1 2026 showed 14% revenue growth and diluted earnings per share of $2.72. At today's price, our base synthesis i...

decision.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

valuation.interpretation: value_per_share is withheld while any material additive component is unpriced; priced_components_per_share is not a complete security value.

scenario_contract.rule: Cases must change cited causal operating, capital, probability, timing, or financing drivers—not only a terminal multiple.

change_control.rule: A source fact is locked. Every assumption change requires a reason, author, timestamp, and before/after value.

optionality.rule: Every option requires a beneficiary, probability, timing, remaining capital, failure case, and non-overlap control; absence of option value must be explicit rather than unvalued.

committee.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

method_fit.label: High-return compounder
applicability_tests: Owner earnings can be separated from stock compensation, working capital, and maintenance capital.
Incremental returns on retained capital are observable and durable.
The competitive advantage explains pricing, retention, share, or cost outcomes.
Terminal value is supported by a reinvestment runway rather than an unexplained multiple.
failure_modes: growth projected without its capital cost
terminal value unsupported by a durable mechanism

validation_cohort.purpose: Test depletion, commodity exposure, finite reserves, and royalty-unit reconciliation.
Test tangible capital, credit losses, funding, regulation, and excess-capital realization.
Test owner earnings, capital-light reinvestment, cyclicality, and buyback value creation.
Test normalized margins, industry supply response, replacement cost, and reinvestment timing.
Test product concentration, pipeline probabilities, patent cliffs, and catalyst timing.

method_fit.rule: Use one primary method and no more than two corroborating methods; irrelevant methods and personas remain silent.

outcomes.weighting_rule: Do not change a persona's relevance within a power zone until at least 20 attributable, dividend-aware outcomes exist there; never reweight automatically.

attribution.explanation: A complete security value is intentionally withheld while material components are unpriced. Attribution restarts after the first proof-complete baseline.

total_return_panel.error: Vendor events are discovery evidence; primary-source completeness has not been established.
total_return_panel.return_contract: split-adjusted close excluding dividends; event amounts normalized for later splits; cash reinvested at ex-date close; pre-tax nominal

coverage.note: Vendor events are discovery evidence; primary-source completeness has not been established.

timeline.label: Net income moved -20.6% versus the prior period (528.0 to 419.0).
Basic EPS moved -19.6% versus the prior period (10.2 to 8.2).
Q1 2026: net sales $1.495B (+14% YoY); operating income $215M; EPS $2.72 diluted
Acquired Elo Holdings for ~$1.30B cash, expanding self-service touchscreen and kiosk portfolio into Connected Frontline segment
Acquired Photoneo (~$62M) for 3D machine vision; results in Asset Visibility and Automation segment
Reorganized reportable segments to Connected Frontline (CF) and Asset Visibility and Automation (AVA), replacing prior EVM/AIT structure
FY2025 share repurchases $587M; cash balance fell to $125M as M&A and buybacks drew down liquidity
Completed Matrox Imaging acquisition, expanding machine vision and fixed industrial scanning in automation adjacency
William J. Burns became CEO, succeeding Anders Gustafsson; Burns had led product and solutions as Chief Product & Solutions Officer since 2015
Acquired Fetch Robotics, entering autonomous mobile robots for warehouse workflows
Acquired Motorola Solutions enterprise business, creating scale in mobile computing and barcode printing

industry.structure: Oligopolistic enterprise asset intelligence market led by Zebra, Honeywell, and Datalogic in mobile computing and automatic identification; high switching costs from validated workflows and installed base.
industry.share_shift: Zebra gaining share in machine vision and self-service via Matrox, Photoneo, and Elo tuck-ins; AVA organic growth turned positive (+7% in FY2025) after prior-year decline.
industry.trend: Mix shift toward services, software subscriptions, and AI-enabled frontline workflows; warehouse automation and RFID adoption remain tailwinds while retail capex cycles create volatility.

risk_claims.claim: Net income moved -20.6% versus the prior period (528.0 to 419.0).
Basic EPS moved -19.6% versus the prior period (10.2 to 8.2).

*Primary risk: Elo integration and leverage. A $1.30 billion cash deal plus $587 million of buybacks drained cash to $125 million while debt rose; failure to lift CF margins or AVA profitability would trap equity between slower growth and deleveraging.
